seoTitle: Apply Vendor Credit in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Record a supplier credit and apply it against an open bill in QuickBooks Online to keep your accounts payable balanced.
intro: When a vendor issues a credit for a return, overpayment, or discount, applying it correctly keeps your accounts payable accurate. This guide shows how to record a Vendor Credit in QuickBooks Online and apply it to an outstanding bill.

steps.heading: Create the vendor credit
steps.body: Click + New, select Vendor Credit, and choose the supplier issuing the credit.

steps.heading: Enter credit details
steps.body: Set the credit date, select the original expense account, and enter the credit amount.

steps.heading: Save the credit
steps.body: Add a memo for your audit trail, then click Save and Close.

steps.heading: Open Pay Bills
steps.body: Click + New again and select Pay Bills, then check the outstanding bill you want to pay.

steps.heading: Apply the credit
steps.body: In the Credits section, check the box next to your recorded credit to reduce your net payment, then save and close.

faq.question: Do I need to create a separate transaction for a vendor credit?
faq.answer: Yes, you first record it as a Vendor Credit, then apply it against a bill through the Pay Bills screen.

faq.question: Can I apply only part of a vendor credit?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply a portion of the credit now and save the remaining balance to apply against a future bill.

faq.question: How do I confirm my vendor's balance updated correctly?
faq.answer: Run the Vendor Balance Detail report to cross-verify the adjusted supplier account.

faq.question: What expense account should the credit use?
faq.answer: Use the same expense category that was used for the original purchase to keep your reporting accurate.
